1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). Background dispatch leaves the parent's session log stuck on "running in background" on the dashboard.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.

Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff

129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

121	private lemma _finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le
122	    {σ : Type*} (s t : σ →₀ ℕ) (i : σ)
123	    (hsub : s - Finsupp.single i 1 = t - Finsupp.single i 1)
124	    (hsi : 1 ≤ s i) (hti : 1 ≤ t i) :
125	    s = t := by
126	  classical
127	  ext j
128	  have hj : (s - Finsupp.single (α := σ) i (1 : ℕ)) j =
129	            (t - Finsupp.single (α := σ) i (1 : ℕ)) j := DFunLike.congr_fun hsub j
130	  rw [Finsupp.tsub_apply, Finsupp.tsub_apply, Finsupp.single_apply] at hj
131	  by_cases hji : j = i
132	  · subst hji; simp at hj; omega
133	  · rw [if_neg (fun h => hji h.symm)] at hj; simpa using hj

135	/-- Coefficient formula for `pderiv`: for `u` with `u i ≥ 1`, the coefficient
136	of `pderiv i f` at `u - single i 1` is exactly `coeff u f * (u i : k)`. -/
137	private lemma _mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted
138	    {σ : Type*} {k : Type*} [CommSemiring k]
139	    (f : MvPolynomial σ k) (i : σ) (u : σ →₀ ℕ) (hui : 1 ≤ u i) :
140	    MvPolynomial.coeff (u - Finsupp.single i 1) ((MvPolynomial.pderiv i) f) =
141	      MvPolynomial.coeff u f * (u i : k) := by
142	  classical
143	  conv_lhs => rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum f]
144	  rw [map_sum, MvPolynomial.coeff_sum]
145	  simp_rw [MvPolynomial.pderiv_monomial, MvPolynomial.coeff_monomial]
146	  by_cases hu : u ∈ f.support
147	  · rw [Finset.sum_eq_single u]
148	    · simp
149	    · intro x _ hxne
150	      by_cases hxsub : x - Finsupp.single i 1 = u - Finsupp.single i 1
151	      · rw [if_pos hxsub]
152	        by_cases hxi : 1 ≤ x i
153	        · exact absurd (_finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le x u i hxsub hxi hui) hxne
154	        · have hxi' : x i = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp (Nat.not_le.mp hxi)
155	          rw [hxi']; simp
156	      · rw [if_neg hxsub]
157	    · intro hu'; exact absurd hu hu'
158	  · rw [MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hu, zero_mul]
159	    apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
160	    intro x hx
161	    by_cases hxsub : x - Finsupp.single i 1 = u - Finsupp.single i 1
162	    · rw [if_pos hxsub]
163	      by_cases hxi : 1 ≤ x i
164	      · exact absurd (by
165	          rw [_finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le x u i hxsub hxi hui] at hx; exact hx) hu
166	      · have hxi' : x i = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp (Nat.not_le.mp hxi)
167	        rw [hxi']; simp
168	    · rw [if_neg hxsub]

170	/-- In `CharZero`, an `MvPolynomial` whose all partial derivatives vanish lies
171	in the image of `MvPolynomial.C`. This is the FREE-CASE of the joint-kernel
172	collapse (BR.5), proven via the coefficient formula `_mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted`.
173	
174	Strategy: We show `f.support ⊆ {0}`, hence `f = monomial 0 (coeff 0 f) = C (coeff 0 f)`.
175	If `s ∈ f.support` with `s ≠ 0`, pick `i` with `s i ≥ 1`. By the coefficient formula,
176	`coeff (s - single i 1) (pderiv i f) = coeff s f * (s i : k)`. The LHS is `0`
177	(by `pderiv i f = 0`), but the RHS is nonzero (`coeff s f ≠ 0` since `s ∈ support`,
178	and `(s i : k) ≠ 0` by `CharZero` + `s i ≥ 1`). Contradiction. -/
179	private lemma _mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_pderiv_eq_zero
180	    {σ : Type*} {k : Type*} [Field k] [CharZero k]
181	    {f : MvPolynomial σ k} (hPDeriv : ∀ i : σ, MvPolynomial.pderiv i f = 0) :
182	    f ∈ (MvPolynomial.C : k →+* MvPolynomial σ k).range := by
183	  classical
184	  suffices hsupp : ∀ s ∈ f.support, s = 0 by
185	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f, ?_⟩
186	    nth_rewrite 2 [MvPolynomial.as_sum f]
187	    rw [show (MvPolynomial.C (MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f) : MvPolynomial σ k) =
188	        MvPolynomial.monomial 0 (MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f) by rfl]
189	    by_cases h0 : 0 ∈ f.support
190	    · have : f.support = {0} := Finset.eq_singleton_iff_unique_mem.mpr ⟨h0, hsupp⟩
191	      rw [this]; simp
192	    · have : f.support = ∅ := by
193	        apply Finset.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem.mpr
194	        intro s hs
195	        rw [hsupp s hs] at hs; exact h0 hs
196	      rw [this, Finset.sum_empty, MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp h0]; simp
197	  intro s hs
198	  by_contra hne
199	  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ : ∃ i, s i ≠ 0 := by
200	    by_contra h
201	    apply hne
202	    ext j
203	    by_contra hj
204	    exact h ⟨j, hj⟩
205	  have hsi : 1 ≤ s i := Nat.one_le_iff_ne_zero.mpr hi
206	  have hc := _mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted f i s hsi
207	  rw [hPDeriv i, MvPolynomial.coeff_zero] at hc
208	  have hcf : MvPolynomial.coeff s f = 0 := by
209	    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hc.symm with h | h
210	    · exact h
211	    · exfalso
212	      have : (s i : ℕ) = 0 := by exact_mod_cast h

478	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
479	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
480	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
481	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
482	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).

485	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. CLOSED iter-153 under the
486	iter-152 alg-closed pivot (`[IsAlgClosed k]`); the proof is three steps:
487	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
488	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IrreducibleSpace X`
489	    (`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`) ⇒
490	    `IsIntegral X` (`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`);
491	    `IsProper` ⇒ `UniversallyClosed` ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
492	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`).
493	(2) `IsProper` (⇒ `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType`) ⇒ the
494	    structure-morphism `appTop` ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` is finite
495	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`), hence `Γ(X, O_X)` is
496	    integral over `k`.
497	(3) `k` algebraically closed ⇒ an integral extension is trivial:
498	    `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives
499	    `algebraMap k Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective, i.e.
500	    `RingHom.range (appTop.hom) = ⊤`. (Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the
501	    former base-change-to-`\bar k` substep collapses entirely — no
502	    `ChartAlgebraS3` separability/inseparability factorisation is used.)

505	snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma `Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field —

508	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
509	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
510	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
511	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
512	    [IsReduced X]
513	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
514	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
515	  -- Iter-153 alg-closed closure. Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the proof
516	  -- collapses to three steps: (1) `X` integral ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` a field;
517	  -- (2) properness ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` finite (hence integral) over `k`;
518	  -- (3) `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives the
519	  -- structure ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective,
520	  -- which is the displayed `range = ⊤`.
521	  set f := X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) with hf
522	  -- (1) `X` is integral: smoothness gives `IsReduced X`, geometric
523	  -- irreducibility over the singleton base `Spec k` gives `IrreducibleSpace X`,
524	  -- and the two combine to `IsIntegral X`.
525	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
526	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f
527	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
528	  -- (1, cont.) properness ⟹ universally closed ⟹ `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field.
529	  letI : Field Γ(X, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField
530	  -- (2) The composite `F : k → Γ(X, ⊤)` (structure map through the
531	  -- canonical `ΓSpecIso`) is finite, hence integral over `k`.
532	  let F := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ f.appTop
533	  have hFinF : F.hom.Finite := by
534	    apply RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2
535	      (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)
536	    exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f
537	  letI : Algebra k Γ(X, ⊤) := F.hom.toAlgebra
538	  haveI : Module.Finite k Γ(X, ⊤) := hFinF
539	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k Γ(X, ⊤) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k Γ(X, ⊤)
540	  -- (3) `k` algebraically closed ⟹ `algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤) = F.hom` is
541	  -- bijective, hence surjective.
542	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤)) :=
543	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
544	  -- Convert the range goal to surjectivity of `f.appTop.hom`; since
545	  -- `F.hom = f.appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom` is surjective, so is
546	  -- `f.appTop.hom`.
547	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
548	  have hFsurj : Function.Surjective ⇑F.hom := hbij.surjective
549	  have hcomp : F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom := rfl
550	  rw [hcomp, RingHom.coe_comp] at hFsurj
551	  exact hFsurj.of_comp

15	the universal derivation `d : K → Ω_{K/k}` is the relative algebraic closure of

17	standing "`k` algebraically closed in `K`" (from `[IsAlgClosed k] + [IsDomain B]`,
18	`K = Frac B`), this gives `x ∈ k`. The content lives in the *transcendental*
19	layer (`Ω_{K/k} ≠ 0`), so unramified/`Ω=0` arguments do not reach it.

56	### Analogue: `Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange` + `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`
57	
58	- **Domain**: cotangent complex / deformation theory (Jacobi–Zariski), crossed
59	  with field-theoretic formal smoothness.
60	- **Same structural problem there**: for a tower `k → F → K`, the sequence
61	  `H¹(L_{K/F}) --δ--> K ⊗_F Ω_{F/k} --mapBaseChange--> Ω_{K/k}` is exact
62	  (`Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange`,
63	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.JacobiZariski`/`CotangentComplex`). When `K/F` is
64	  formally smooth, `H¹(L_{K/F})` is `Subsingleton`
65	  (`instSubsingletonH1CotangentOfFormallySmooth`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`),
66	  so `mapBaseChange k F K` is **injective**. This is exactly the directive's
67	  desired LEFT-exactness of `Ω_{F/k} ⊗ K → Ω_{K/k}`.
68	- **Why it applies without a transcendence basis**: take `F = k(b)`. In char 0,
69	  `F` is a **perfect field** (`PerfectField.ofCharZero`, `Mathlib.FieldTheory.Perfect`),
70	  and `K` is essentially of finite type over `F`
71	  (`Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_comp` from `EssFiniteType k K`,
72	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.EssentialFiniteType`). Then
73	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field`)
74	  gives `FormallySmooth F K` directly — **no separating transcendence basis, no
75	  "all partials vanish" combinatorics, no freeness-of-Ω-on-a-basis step.**
76	- **Technique**: `mapBaseChange k F K (1 ⊗ D_F x) = D_K (algebraMap F K x)` via
77	  `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange_tmul` + `map_D` (`one_smul`). So
78	  `D_K b = 0 ⟹ 1 ⊗ D_F b = 0 ⟹ D_F b = 0` by faithful flatness
79	  (`Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`; the instance `FaithfullyFlat F K`
80	  for a field extension resolves by `inferInstance`). Contradiction with the base
81	  case below ⟹ `b` is algebraic over `k`.
82	- **Mapping to project**: in
83	  `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`, first push `D_B b = 0`
84	  to `D_K b = 0` (`K = FractionRing B`) via `map_D` (the project already has this
85	  as `_hFunct`). Then run the contradiction with `F` realized as
86	  `FractionRing (Polynomial k)` embedded into `K` by `b` (see base case).
87	- **Porting cost**: **low–medium**. All lemmas verified to compose; the work is
88	  scalar-tower/instance plumbing.
89	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.

91	### Analogue: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` + `polynomialEquiv_D` (transcendental base case)
92	
93	- **Domain**: localization / essentially-étale base change of Kähler differentials.
94	- **Same structural problem there**: a localization `S → T` is formally étale
95	  (`Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isLocalization`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Basic`),
96	  so `Ω_{T/k}` is the localized module of `Ω_{S/k}`
97	  (`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Localization`).
98	- **Technique (FULLY verified, ~20 LOC)**: to show `D_{k(t)} t ≠ 0` for `t`
99	  transcendental, take `S = k[X]`, `T = FractionRing k[X]`. From `D_T(t)=0`,
100	  `map k k S T (D_S X) = 0`, so by `IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff` there is
101	  `c ∈ nonZeroDivisors` with `c • D_S X = 0`. Apply the `k[X]`-linear iso
102	  `KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv` (`polynomialEquiv_D : polynomialEquiv (D X) = derivative X = 1`):
103	  `c • 1 = 0` in `k[X]`, contradicting `nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`. No
104	  `NoZeroSMulDivisors` lemma even needed.

/-- (FT.3 base case) The universal Kähler derivation does not kill the
transcendental generator: `D (X) ≠ 0` in `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]`. Verified route
(analogist iter-154): `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]` is the localization of `Ω[k[X]⁄k]`
(`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`, formally-étale localization),
so `D X = 0` would give `c • D_{k[X]} X = 0` for some non-zero-divisor `c`;
transporting along the `k[X]`-linear iso `polynomialEquiv` (with
`polynomialEquiv_D` ↦ `derivative X = 1`) yields `c • 1 = 0`, contradicting
`nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`. -/
private lemma _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero {k : Type*} [Field k] :
    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) Polynomial.X) ≠ 0 := by
  intro h
  have hmap : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
      (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (Polynomial k) Polynomial.X) = 0 := by
    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]; exact h
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
        (FractionRing (Polynomial k))) :=
    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map _
  rw [IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))] at hmap
  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hmap
  rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hc
  have hc1 := congrArg (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv (R := k)) hc
  rw [map_smul, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv_D, Polynomial.derivative_X,
    map_zero, smul_eq_mul, mul_one] at hc1
  exact nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero c hc1

/-- (FT.3 closer) Over an algebraically closed base field `k`, an element of a
`k`-algebra field `K` that is algebraic over `k` lies in the image of
`algebraMap k K`. Verified route (analogist iter-154): `k⟮b⟯` is finite over `k`
(`IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional`), hence integral, so
`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` makes `algebraMap k k⟮b⟯`
surjective; pull the generator back. -/
private lemma _algebraic_mem_range {k K : Type*} [Field k] [Field K] [IsAlgClosed k]
    [Algebra k K] {b : K} (hb : IsAlgebraic k b) :
    b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range := by
  haveI : FiniteDimensional k k⟮b⟯ := IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional hb.isIntegral
  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k k⟮b⟯ := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k k⟮b⟯
  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := k) (K := k⟮b⟯)).surjective
      (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen k b)
  exact ⟨c, by
    have := congrArg (algebraMap k⟮b⟯ K) hc
    rwa [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.algebraMap_gen]
      at this⟩

  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
  -- ===========================================================================
  -- iter-154 closure: the verified single-element / perfect-field /
  -- Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
  -- `analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`). The standard-smooth structure is
  -- NOT used by this argument (only `[IsDomain B]` + `[FiniteType k B]` to
  -- build `K = Frac B` and `EssFiniteType k K`); `n` and standard-smoothness
  -- remain harmless ambient hypotheses.
  -- ===========================================================================
  -- (FT.1) Push `D_B b = 0` forward to the fraction field `K = Frac B`.
  set K := FractionRing B with hK

    rw [← _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D k k B K b, hDb, map_zero]
  -- It suffices to put `algebraMap B K b ∈ range(algebraMap k K)`; pull back
  -- along the injective `algebraMap B K` (domain ↪ fraction field).
  suffices hbK : algebraMap B K b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range by
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hbK
    refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
    apply IsFractionRing.injective B K
    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k B K]
    exact hc
  -- Reduce to algebraicity of `bK := algebraMap B K b` over `k`, then close
  -- with `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`).
  apply _algebraic_mem_range
  -- (FT.2) Suppose, for contradiction, that `bK` is transcendental over `k`.
  by_contra hCon
  have hTr : Transcendental k (algebraMap B K b) := hCon
  have hInj : Function.Injective ⇑(Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) :=
    transcendental_iff_injective.mp hTr
  -- Embed `F = Frac(k[X])` into `K` by `X ↦ bK` via `IsFractionRing.lift`.
  set F := FractionRing (Polynomial k) with hF

    fun x => IsFractionRing.lift_algebraMap hg x

  -- exact sequence + `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)` from formal smoothness.

/-! ### (BR.5) helper machinery — MvPolynomial joint-kernel collapse

The (BR.5) joint-kernel collapse argument for the KDM lemma below is built on
two helper facts about `MvPolynomial σ k` in `CharZero`:

1. The coefficient formula for `pderiv`: for `u : σ →₀ ℕ` with `u i ≥ 1`,
   `coeff (u - single i 1) (pderiv i f) = coeff u f * (u i : k)`.

2. The joint-kernel-equals-constants fact for the free polynomial algebra: in
   `CharZero`, if all `pderiv i f = 0` then `f ∈ MvPolynomial.C.range`.

The (BR.5) closure path for a standard-smooth `B` then proceeds by lifting `b`
to `b̃ ∈ MvPolynomial ι k` via the `SubmersivePresentation`, transferring the
`D b = 0` hypothesis through the functoriality `Ω[MvPoly⁄k] → Ω[B⁄k]`, and
applying helper 2 — modulo the technical step of choosing a lift `b̃` whose
own `D b̃` vanishes in `Ω[MvPoly⁄k]` (not just in the quotient). The transfer
step depends on a Mathlib-side observation about `KaehlerDifferential.ker_map_of_surjective`
that is not directly packaged in `b80f227`; the iter-150 HYBRID (C) commitment
lands the helpers and the strategic scaffold, with the transfer-step Mathlib
gap recorded inline at the `sorry` point. -/

private lemma _finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le
    {σ : Type*} (s t : σ →₀ ℕ) (i : σ)
    (hsub : s - Finsupp.single i 1 = t - Finsupp.single i 1)
    (hsi : 1 ≤ s i) (hti : 1 ≤ t i) :
    s = t := by
  classical
  ext j
  have hj : (s - Finsupp.single (α := σ) i (1 : ℕ)) j =
            (t - Finsupp.single (α := σ) i (1 : ℕ)) j := DFunLike.congr_fun hsub j
  rw [Finsupp.tsub_apply, Finsupp.tsub_apply, Finsupp.single_apply] at hj
  by_cases hji : j = i
  · subst hji; simp at hj; omega
  · rw [if_neg (fun h => hji h.symm)] at hj; simpa using hj

/-- Coefficient formula for `pderiv`: for `u` with `u i ≥ 1`, the coefficient
of `pderiv i f` at `u - single i 1` is exactly `coeff u f * (u i : k)`. -/
private lemma _mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted
    {σ : Type*} {k : Type*} [CommSemiring k]
    (f : MvPolynomial σ k) (i : σ) (u : σ →₀ ℕ) (hui : 1 ≤ u i) :
    MvPolynomial.coeff (u - Finsupp.single i 1) ((MvPolynomial.pderiv i) f) =
      MvPolynomial.coeff u f * (u i : k) := by
  classical
  conv_lhs => rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum f]
  rw [map_sum, MvPolynomial.coeff_sum]
  simp_rw [MvPolynomial.pderiv_monomial, MvPolynomial.coeff_monomial]
  by_cases hu : u ∈ f.support
  · rw [Finset.sum_eq_single u]
    · simp
    · intro x _ hxne
      by_cases hxsub : x - Finsupp.single i 1 = u - Finsupp.single i 1
      · rw [if_pos hxsub]
        by_cases hxi : 1 ≤ x i
        · exact absurd (_finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le x u i hxsub hxi hui) hxne
        · have hxi' : x i = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp (Nat.not_le.mp hxi)
          rw [hxi']; simp
      · rw [if_neg hxsub]
    · intro hu'; exact absurd hu hu'
  · rw [MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hu, zero_mul]
    apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
    intro x hx
    by_cases hxsub : x - Finsupp.single i 1 = u - Finsupp.single i 1
    · rw [if_pos hxsub]
      by_cases hxi : 1 ≤ x i
      · exact absurd (by
          rw [_finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le x u i hxsub hxi hui] at hx; exact hx) hu
      · have hxi' : x i = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp (Nat.not_le.mp hxi)
        rw [hxi']; simp
    · rw [if_neg hxsub]

/-- In `CharZero`, an `MvPolynomial` whose all partial derivatives vanish lies
in the image of `MvPolynomial.C`. This is the FREE-CASE of the joint-kernel
collapse (BR.5), proven via the coefficient formula `_mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted`.

Strategy: We show `f.support ⊆ {0}`, hence `f = monomial 0 (coeff 0 f) = C (coeff 0 f)`.
If `s ∈ f.support` with `s ≠ 0`, pick `i` with `s i ≥ 1`. By the coefficient formula,
`coeff (s - single i 1) (pderiv i f) = coeff s f * (s i : k)`. The LHS is `0`
(by `pderiv i f = 0`), but the RHS is nonzero (`coeff s f ≠ 0` since `s ∈ support`,
and `(s i : k) ≠ 0` by `CharZero` + `s i ≥ 1`). Contradiction. -/
private lemma _mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_pderiv_eq_zero
    {σ : Type*} {k : Type*} [Field k] [CharZero k]
    {f : MvPolynomial σ k} (hPDeriv : ∀ i : σ, MvPolynomial.pderiv i f = 0) :
    f ∈ (MvPolynomial.C : k →+* MvPolynomial σ k).range := by
  classical
  suffices hsupp : ∀ s ∈ f.support, s = 0 by
    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f, ?_⟩
    nth_rewrite 2 [MvPolynomial.as_sum f]
    rw [show (MvPolynomial.C (MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f) : MvPolynomial σ k) =
        MvPolynomial.monomial 0 (MvPolynomial.coeff 0 f) by rfl]
    by_cases h0 : 0 ∈ f.support
    · have : f.support = {0} := Finset.eq_singleton_iff_unique_mem.mpr ⟨h0, hsupp⟩
      rw [this]; simp
    · have : f.support = ∅ := by
        apply Finset.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem.mpr
        intro s hs
        rw [hsupp s hs] at hs; exact h0 hs
      rw [this, Finset.sum_empty, MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp h0]; simp
  intro s hs
  by_contra hne
  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ : ∃ i, s i ≠ 0 := by
    by_contra h
    apply hne
    ext j
    by_contra hj
    exact h ⟨j, hj⟩
  have hsi : 1 ≤ s i := Nat.one_le_iff_ne_zero.mpr hi
  have hc := _mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted f i s hsi
  rw [hPDeriv i, MvPolynomial.coeff_zero] at hc
  have hcf : MvPolynomial.coeff s f = 0 := by
    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hc.symm with h | h
    · exact h
    · exfalso
      have : (s i : ℕ) = 0 := by exact_mod_cast h
      omega
  exact MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hs hcf

/-- The KDM (BR.5) FREE-CASE: in `CharZero`, the universal Kähler derivation
`D : MvPolynomial σ k → Ω[MvPolynomial σ k⁄k]` has kernel exactly
`MvPolynomial.C.range`. -/
private lemma _mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_D_eq_zero
    {σ : Type*} {k : Type*} [Field k] [CharZero k]
    {f : MvPolynomial σ k}
    (hDf : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (MvPolynomial σ k) f = 0) :
    f ∈ (MvPolynomial.C : k →+* MvPolynomial σ k).range := by
  apply _mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_pderiv_eq_zero
  intro i
  have h := KaehlerDifferential.mvPolynomialBasis_repr_apply k σ f i
  rw [hDf] at h
  simp at h
  exact h.symm
